Definition: Monitoring of user activity or system behaviour for security or analytical purposes. PointSav replaces third-party surveillance dependencies with zero-cookie first-party telemetry pipelines.
A term describing structural dependency on platform providers who control access to data and services. Cited as context for the vendor lock-in problem that PointSav is designed to resolve.

What You See Is What You Get or WYSIWYG

Definition: The stateless logic and compute layer. Holds no data. Connects ConsoleOS terminals to one or more ToteboxOS archives and provides extended compute capacity for BIM rendering
column6: and data warehouse operations. Required for multi-archive use cases — the monetisation boundary between free and proprietary tiers.

Definition: A ToteboxOS archive for a physical property. Anchored to a Land Title PIN or legal address. Contains permits
column8: and maintenance history. Replaces legacy term RealPropertyArchive.

Definition: The deterministic parser service. Strips proprietary formatting from inbound payloads and routes structured data to deterministic services and unstructured text to service-slm. Assigns transaction IDs for chain-of-custody tracing. Canonical long-term name replacing the legacy working name service-parser.

The inverted index service. Provides rapid full-text retrieval across all files in a Totebox Archive using Tantivy. Operates without a running database engine — the index is static and binary and can be searched on an air-gapped machine. Satisfies DARP compliance: data must be searchable without proprietary software.
